title: Unit 4 Checkpoint Quiz — Complex Sentences & Connectors

questions.explanation: 'Por lo tanto' (therefore) expresses consequence — it introduces a result. 'Sin embargo' = contrast, 'ya que' = cause, 'a pesar de que' = concession.
'Sino' is used after a negation to replace/correct: 'No es músico sino médico' = He's not a musician but rather a doctor. 'Pero' would add a contrasting idea, not replace the negated one.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'como' correctly as a cause connector?
questions.explanation: When 'como' means 'since/because' (cause), it must come at the START of the sentence: 'Como estaba enfermo, no vine.' If it appears mid-sentence, 'como' means 'like/as' or introduces a comparison.

questions.prompt: Which connector is most appropriate for FORMAL written Spanish?
questions.explanation: 'No obstante' (nevertheless) is a formal connector equivalent to 'sin embargo'. 'O sea' and 'es más' are colloquial/neutral, and 'así que' is neutral but informal. For formal writing, prefer 'no obstante', 'por lo tanto', 'asimismo', 'si bien'.

questions.prompt: Complete: Trabajó mucho; ___, no terminó a tiempo.
questions.explanation: The sentence contrasts hard work with the failure to finish — a contrast connector is needed. 'Sin embargo' (however) correctly introduces this contradiction. 'Así que' would introduce a consequence, not a contrast.
'Ni siquiera' means 'not even' — it adds strong emphasis to a negation. 'No llamó' = he didn't call. 'Ni siquiera llamó' = he didn't even call (stronger, more emphatic).

questions.prompt: Which pair of connectors is used to present two balanced contrasting points?
options: por un lado... por otro lado
questions.explanation: 'Por un lado... por otro lado' (on one hand... on the other hand) is the standard structure for presenting two contrasting aspects of a single issue. 'Primero/luego' sequences events; 'además/también' adds ideas; 'ya que/por lo tanto' links cause and effect.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'a pesar de que':
options: A pesar de que llegó tarde, lo perdonamos.
A pesar de que llegó tarde, ya que lo perdonamos.
Llegó tarde a pesar de que lo perdonamos.
A pesar de que, llegó tarde.
questions.explanation: 'A pesar de que' (despite the fact that) introduces a concession — an acknowledged obstacle that doesn't prevent the main action. 'A pesar de que llegó tarde, lo perdonamos' = Despite the fact that he was late, we forgave him.
